summary: The plan keeps TASK-0087 as one coherent trust-boundary ticket, replaces the hidden completion-gate review with a visible nonce-backed receipt flow, and names the concrete file seams, data shapes, and proof path needed to build it safely.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The ticket stays one coherent build-and-proof loop instead of fake-splitting stop-hook mechanics from the receipt contract.
Acceptance criteria and verification make the nonce and receipt boundary observable rather than implied.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep the implementation focused on the completion receipt contract and avoid broad hook-side orchestration redesign.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
findings: The plan names the real code seams and keeps the Stop hook deterministic instead of hand-waving about visible subagents inside Python.
The typed flow example and ordered execution steps make the nonce-backed receipt path implementable without inventing missing behavior.
rubric_sections.next_action: During build, preserve the mechanical-first ordering: request nonce, require visible receipt, then validate receipt before completion.

next_action: Approve TASK-0087 for build if the visible nonce-backed completion review receipt is the chosen direction.
